preamble: The insertion of a near-open front vowel [æ] from an empty state is a specific form of prothesis or epenthesis. While languages typically prefer neutral vowels like schwa [ə] or high vowels [i] to break up illicit consonant clusters, [æ] can appear due to specific phonetic environments or L2 transfer.

examples.note: Prothesis: Farsi speakers often insert an [æ] or [ə] before English s-clusters, which are not permitted in Farsi phonotactics.
Prothesis of a low front/central vowel before initial rhotics.
